---
title: "Community"
author: "David Rach"
format: html
toc: true
toc-depth: 5
---

![](/images/WebsiteBanner.png)

::: {style="text-align: right;"}
[![AGPL-3.0](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-AGPLv3-blue)](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl-3.0.en.html) [![CC BY-SA 4.0](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-CC%20BY--SA%204.0-lightgrey.svg)](http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/)
:::

Beyond the "Cytometry in R" course, there are a lot of cytometry-focused R packages on Bioconductor, GitHub and CRAN. While covering them all during the course is not possible, as time allows, we will include small walkthroughs for individual packages. 

These walk-throughs are intended to supplement (not surplant) the existing package vignettes, while providing the small datasets and contextual details more typical of the "Cytometry in R" course to help accelerate the packages utilization by beginners. 

Check back sporadically for additional contributions. 

![](/images/YouTubeHex.png){width="50%"}

::: {style="text-align: right;"}
[![AGPL-3.0](https://www.gnu.org/graphics/agplv3-with-text-162x68.png)](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl-3.0.en.html) [![CC BY-SA 4.0](https://licensebuttons.net/l/by-sa/4.0/88x31.png)](http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/)
:::
